last_assistant_message:
19/19 pass. Task list reminder is for the user's loop runner, not relevant to my read-only verification. Verification complete.

---

**Spec compliance: PASS**

All 10 verification points hold:

1. `tests/ingest-retry.test.mjs` exists, 169 lines, three test cases.
2. Test 1 (lines 100-119): server always returns 503, asserts `srv.calls.count === 2` (line 115) against a per-server counter incremented in `createServer` handler (line 39). Counter-based, no time/sleep assertion.
3. Test 2 (lines 121-140): server always returns 400, asserts `srv.calls.count === 1` (line 136).
4. Test 3 (lines 142-169): server returns 503 on first call, 200 on second, asserts `srv.calls.count === 2` (line 165). Response body is a valid ingest response.
5. Bearer is faked safely. `fakePluginRoot()` (lines 54-62) builds a temp dir with `bin/memory-refresh` that echoes `fake-bearer-token`; `runHook` passes `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT: pluginRoot` so the helper's `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/memory-refresh` call (helper line 77) targets the stub. Real `bin/memory-refresh` and `~/.config/memory/auth.json` are not touched.
6. Servers close on every path: each test has a `try { ... } finally { await srv.close(); }` block (lines 106-118, 127-139, 156-168). `withServer.close` wraps `srv.close` in a promise (line 48).
7. Async dispatch: uses `spawn` (line 89) wrapped in a Promise that resolves on `child.on('close')` (line 95). Comment at lines 17-23 documents why `spawnSync` would deadlock against the same-process HTTP server. Node event loop stays unblocked.
8. `node --test tests/ingest-retry.test.mjs` → 3/3 pass.
9. `node --test tests/*.test.mjs` → 19/19 pass.
10. Commit `a8c57c8` is on `feat/transcript-ingest`, test-style message `test(plugin): retry budget honors MEMORY_INGEST_RETRIES`, touches only `tests/ingest-retry.test.mjs` (169 insertions).

Relevant files: `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\tests\ingest-retry.test.mjs`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\hooks\scripts\_ingest-transcript.sh`.
